title: Fulstar plate
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Device |image=[[File:WrongPlates-AICAS.jpg]] |name=Fulstar plate |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Hyperdrive]] component''[[Star Wars Adventures in Colors and Shapes]]'' |culture= |creators= |created= |modifier= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners= |locations= |cost= |value= |structure= |size= |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Excuse me, Captain Solo, but is that plate important?''"
"''Important? Listen, professor, the first time we try to go into hyperspace without it, we'll end up as space dust!|C-3PO and Captain Solo, discussing fulstar plates|audio=FulstarPlatesAreImportant.flac|}} '''Fulstar plates''' were [[Magnet/Legends|magnetized]] metal components critical to [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] use. Jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] without one equipped would result in destruction. Distinguished by shape and [[Color/Legends|color]], each [[Starship/Legends|starship]] class required a particular style of plate. ==Characteristics== Fulstar plates were [[Magnet/Legends|magnetized]] metal plates available in various shapes and [[Color/Legends|colors]]. They were small enough to be held by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] in one [[Limb/Legends|hand]], and the face of each plate was marked by a pattern of dots and lines. Part of the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] system, jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] without one would cause a vessel's destruction. Each [[Starship/Legends|starship]] class required a specific style—plates with round sides only worked in [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], and the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] required a square, red plate. ==History== At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] [[Counterpart/Legends|counterpart]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] attempted to assist the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Executive officer/Legends|first mate]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] with repairs to their [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]], the ''Millennium Falcon'', by locating a red, square fulstar plate that [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Solo needed. A supply room aboard the ''Falcon'' held square plates in multiple colors, but C-3PO's [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] were damaged when he knocked over some supplies, and he could not identify the correct color plate. After a process of trial and error, and help from the ''Falcon''{{'s}} central [[Computer/Legends|computer]], he brought the correct plate to Solo. The plate was then installed and the hyperdrive fixed. Rebel [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermasters]] kept round fulstar plates on hand, and the [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] [[Zilnor]] was a supplier of triangular and pentagonal fulstar plates. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:FulstarPlates.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Falcon'' evades TIE fighters amongst a cloud of plates.]] Fulstar plates were introduced in [[1984]]'s ''[[Star Wars Adventures in Colors and Shapes]]'', an educational [[Read-Along Adventures]] book-and-record for young readers published by [[Buena Vista Records]]. The cover of the book depicts C-3PO and R2-D2 alone in the cockpit of the ''Millennium Falcon'' being pursued by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] in the midst of a cloud of random shapes, most likely the fulstar plates around which the story revolves. This event is not depicted in the narrative. Fulstar plates were going to be featured in [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[Adventures in Hyperspace: The Big Switch|''The Big Switch'']], the third book in the young-readers series ''[[Star Wars: Adventures in Hyperspace]]''.{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarstimeline.net/AdventuresinHyperspace.htm|text=Star Wars Adventures in Hyperspace Book 3 The Big Switch|author=van der Heijden, Eddie|date=2015-04-22|work=[http://www.starwarstimeline.net The Star Wars Expanded Universe Timeline]|archivedate=20161030010051}} The book, however, went unpublished after it was announced that the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] had been rebranded as [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], and that new content within past Legends series would not be published.{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} ==Appearances== *''[[Adventures in Hyperspace: The Big Switch]]'' {{C|Canceled}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Adventures in Colors and Shapes]]'' {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of fulstar plates}} {{Reflist}} [[Category:Hyperdrive parts]] [[Category:Magnetic technology]]
